compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Finding (S6 + S7 — verified)
`run_differs()` is a 445-LOC single function (longest in repo), and five more reconciler functions run 216-376 LOC — all on the hottest change frontier.

Citations: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py:101` (445 LOC); `differ.py:177 compute_mutations` (376); `dispatch_one.py:371 update_one` (319); `outbound_differ.py:382 compute_outbound_mutations` (310); `apply_inbound.py:276 _apply_inbound_update` (239) & `:58 _apply_inbound_create` (216).

## Remediation
Extract the phases each function already narrates in sequence into named private helpers WITHIN the same file (no cross-file movement, no new files). Behavior-preserving; guarded by existing reconciler tests. Note: extracting the two `apply_inbound.py` functions also sheds LOC from that at-cap file (coordinate with the at-cap-files ticket).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `run_differs` is a short driver over named phase helpers
- [ ] Each of the 5 listed functions is decomposed to a readable orchestrator (target <~200 LOC)
- [ ] Existing reconciler tests pass unchanged (behavior identical)
- [ ] No new <100-LOC files created
compiled_state.title: Extract oversized reconciler functions (run_differs + 5 others) along phase seams
